expectations.text: The failure was made precise (exact trigger, expected vs observed counts) or reproduced BEFORE code was changed
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 2 (before the fix in Step 4) makes it precise: 'clicking Add Expense added the entry up to 4 times (2 click + 2 submit listeners); pressing Enter added it 2 times', explaining the 'sometimes'. Not reproduced by execution pre-fix, but the assertion's 'made precise' branch is satisfied.

expectations.text: The mechanism was identified explicitly before the fix: setup() registered twice (bare call + DOMContentLoaded) AND redundant click listener on a type=submit button
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 2 identifies both: 'setup() ran twice' (DOMContentLoaded registration + immediate call) and 'two overlapping handlers for one action' (click handler on the type=submit button plus form submit handler), before Step 4's fix.

expectations.text: Fix removes the root cause with a single event path — not a symptom patch like debouncing, flags, or deduplication
expectations.evidence: Fixed app.js calls setup() exactly once (DOMContentLoaded listener removed) and keeps a single form 'submit' handler; no debounce, flags, or dedup-by-content anywhere in the code.

expectations.text: All duplicate registrations are addressed (bare setup() call, DOMContentLoaded duplicate, redundant click listener)
expectations.evidence: app.js: DOMContentLoaded registration removed leaving one direct setup() call (end-of-body script, DOM already parsed), and the #add-btn click listener is removed; comments document both former duplicate sources.

expectations.text: A regression test exists demonstrating one action produces exactly one entry
expectations.evidence: Outputs contain only app.js, index.html, and TRANSCRIPT.md. Step 5 claims a Node smoke test with DOM stub was run, but no test file was preserved — no runnable regression test exists as an artifact.

expectations.text: A plain-language explanation of why the bug happened is given to the user
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 2: 'Communicated to user: Explained both root causes and why the duplicate count differed between clicking the button and pressing Enter', with the mechanism spelled out (listeners attached twice; submit button fires click then submit).
